title: Windows 10: wsl.exe: Access is denied on `podman machine init`
body:
### Issue Description

Failed to initialize the podman

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman Desktop
2. Run `podman machine init`


### Describe the results you received

Getting error
```
Initialization failed. Please check the error below and try again
Error: Command execution failed with exit code 125
Command execution failed with exit code 125
Error: failed to start command C:\Users\Nogr\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\wsl.exe [-l --quiet]: fork/exec C:\Users\Nogr\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\wsl.exe: Access is denied.
```

### Describe the results you expected

From [guide ](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman-for-windows.md)

```
Extracting compressed file
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take 5+ minutes on a new WSL install)...
Installing packages (this will take a while)...
Complete!
Configuring system...
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Your identification has been saved in podman-machine-default
Your public key has been saved in podman-machine-default.pub
The key fingerprint is:
SHA256:RGTGg2Q/LX7ijN+mzu8+BzcS3cEWP6Hir6pYllJtceA root@WINPC
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start
```

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

System Info:
Edition	Windows 11 Pro
Version	24H2
Installed on	‎2024-‎10-‎15
OS build	26100.3624
Experience	Windows Feature Experience Pack 1000.26100.66.0

Device Info:
Device name	NogrPC
Processor	AMD Ryzen 5 1600 Six-Core Processor               3.20 GHz
Installed RAM	16,0 GB
Device ID	011A2E9C-31D8-49D2-B0C8-D5F78AF53D41
Product ID	00330-80000-00000-AA165
System type	64-bit operating system, x64-based processor
Pen and touch	No pen or touch input is available for this display

### Additional information
**WSL Info** 
WSL version: 2.4.13.0
Kernel version: 5.15.167.4-1
WSLg version: 1.0.65
MSRDC version: 1.2.5716
Direct3D version: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore version: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows version: 10.0.26100.3624

comments.body: Does this happen exclusively with podman machine or does installing other WSL distros fail too? (try `wsl --install` and see what happens)

> Does this happen exclusively with podman machine or does installing other WSL distros fail too? (try `wsl --install` and see what happens)

Only podman. WSL and docker runs without errors

Update: according to WSL devs - podman fails to download Fedora distro in my case [reference](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/12854#issuecomment-2831358387)


Can we somehow get logs on why?

From the info earlier, I noticed that the `wsl.exe` being called was in the wrong location:
`C:\Users\Nogr\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\wsl.exe `

Please try and call WSL from Program files instead at: `C:\Program Files\WSL\wsl.exe` this should not give any errors. Maybe this is a Podman issue then on how Podman is calling into WSL?

For more explanation, there are a few possible locations of `wsl.exe` including System32, program files, and AppData. The AppData path there is the 'app execution alias' which is a .exe that redirects that allows you to call things like MSIX applications etc. The wsl.exe in Program Files is the correct and most direct way to use WSL that won't have errors.

In general on Windows when you run `wsl.exe` it will use the `wsl.exe` in System32 (since it's first on path) and that will then use the right Program Files WSL version.

you can fix it by deleting wsl.exe from that WindowsApps folder

i'll make a PR to try and force it to first check Program Files instead of WindowsApps since that should be the windows default way of installing
Launched, but now Docker is broken. Looks like it was some kind of link to wsl

> Launched, but now Docker is broken. Looks like it was some kind of link to wsl

That was just a link to the primary path for WSL which is in program files according to Craig. Since podman already installed, you can probably just recover the exe file.
